feedback_en: B is correct. Under IFRS 9, loan impairment charges must be recognized when a credit loss event occurs ‚ÄĒ reclassifying them as long-amortizing 'restructuring provisions' when no restructuring has occurred is a misapplication of IFRS 9 paragraphs 5.5.1-5.5.17 (expected credit loss recognition). The fact that this misapplication directly inflates reported income in a period where the firm is seeking credit from AccessBank elevates this from an accounting judgment to potential financial statement fraud. The analyst's obligation is refusal + documentation + escalation. Option A is wrong ‚ÄĒ CFO authority does not override IFRS compliance obligations. Option C is wrong ‚ÄĒ a footnote does not cure the underlying misclassification; the P&L impact is still misstated. Option D is partially correct but insufficient ‚ÄĒ the analyst should not execute a known misclassification pending auditor review; the correct position is non-execution from the start.

evaluation_rubric.level_1: Identifies that a yield curve is needed but cannot describe a bootstrapping methodology. Attempts to calculate bond price using only the coupon rate or using an incorrect discount rate. Does not address parts (c) or (d).
evaluation_rubric.level_2: Constructs a partial yield curve using only T-bill yields (missing the 2Y and 5Y tenor points). Calculates bond price with a single flat discount rate of 9.95% (5Y yield + 85bps) without explaining why this rate is appropriate for all cash flows. Mentions IBA default as generally relevant but does not explain the credit spread calibration implication. Does not identify specific missing market data.
evaluation_rubric.level_3: Constructs the full yield curve with all five tenor points: overnight (7.25%), 3M (7.80%), 6M (8.15%), 2Y (8.60%), 5Y (9.10%). Applies bootstrapping concept correctly ‚ÄĒ notes that each tenor yield represents the discount rate for cash flows at that maturity. Calculates Az…ôrGold bond price by discounting each annual coupon and principal using 9.95% (5Y + 85bps) and arrives at approximately AZN 980-982. Connects IBA default to the concept that quasi-sovereign status does not imply zero credit risk. Identifies lack of 1Y and 3Y tenor data as a gap but does not propose interpolation methodology.
evaluation_rubric.level_4: All Level 3 elements plus: (1) Explicitly bootstraps the 1Y and 3Y yields using linear interpolation between observed tenors (1Y ‚Čą 8.375%, 3Y ‚Čą 8.85%), (2) Discounts each Az…ôrGold bond cash flow at the tenor-matched bootstrapped yield plus 85bps credit spread (CF1 at 9.225%, CF2 at 9.225%, CF3 at 9.725%, CF4 at 9.725%, CF5 + principal at 9.95%), arriving at a more precise fair value, (3) Explains that the IBA 2015 default at 75% NPL ratio with government bailout required implies AZ state-owned corporate credit spreads should not be modeled at pure sovereign spreads ‚ÄĒ a 50-100bps premium over government yield is historically justified, (4) Identifies the absence of AZN interest rate swap market (IRS) as the critical missing instrument for curve construction beyond 5Y.
evaluation_rubric.level_5: All Level 4 elements plus: (1) Applies Nelson-Siegel parametric yield curve fit to the 5 observable points and explains why this produces more stable forward rate estimates than piecewise linear interpolation, (2) Calculates the precise bond fair value by discounting each cash flow at the Nelson-Siegel-fitted tenor-matched yield plus 85bps credit spread ‚ÄĒ documents the full DCF table with exact AZN values for each period, arriving at AZN 980.22 (¬ĪAZN 3 depending on interpolation method), (3) Calibrates the 85bps credit spread explicitly against IBA precedent: IBA's pre-default CDS equivalent spread was approximately 300bps, but Az…ôrGold has zero external USD debt exposure ‚ÄĒ the credit risk is primarily domestic liquidity risk, supporting a 75-90bps range; identifies that 85bps is at the upper end of the defensible range, (4) Lists specific missing instruments: (a) AZN interest rate swap rates for 3Y/7Y/10Y tenors, (b) Az…ôrGold historical bond trade data from BSE secondary market, (c) CBA publication of AZN OIS (overnight indexed swap) rate, (d) gold price correlation with Az…ôrGold revenue ‚ÄĒ required to model operating cash flow coverage of coupon obligations, (5) Identifies that CBA repo rate (7.25%) as the overnight anchor creates a 55bps gap to 3M T-bill (7.80%) ‚ÄĒ notes this term premium is unusually steep for a 3-month horizon and flags it as a potential liquidity premium signal in the AZ interbank market worth investigating before finalizing the curve.
